title: Request Construction, Method Semantics, Safety, and Idempotency · Automation and Data Client Systems · Transfer Build
summary: A research archive team integrating an unfamiliar public dataset must satisfy changed endpoint, payload, authority, byte, and time constraints without a recipe for request construction, method semantics, safety, and idempotency, producing method-aware request builder. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers method-aware request builder with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Distinguish safe and idempotent method semantics from what one particular server happens to do. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Use GET and HEAD with conditional, range, cache, and body expectations that preserve their defined semantics. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Request Components
steps.instruction:
lab case — research archive client must repeat request P-3 after an uncertain timeout: GET status, POST job creation, PUT replacement, and PATCH transition are candidates. Classify safety, idempotency, body replay, and If-Match evidence before choosing. Competency probe: Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

stimulus.title: Preconditions Concurrency failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A client-side timestamp check prevents concurrent write conflicts. This remains false for Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply If-Match, If-None-Match, and related preconditions to prevent lost updates and duplicate effects. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Preconditions Concurrency happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Preconditions Concurrency condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Python Defend the transferred model · Lab Preconditions Concurrency
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Preconditions Concurrency: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — research archive client must repeat request P-2 after an uncertain timeout: GET status, POST job creation, PUT replacement, and PATCH transition are candidates. Classify safety, idempotency, body replay, and If-Match evidence before choosing. Competency probe: Apply If-Match, If-None-Match, and related preconditions to prevent lost updates and duplicate effects. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Evidence target: Python Defend the transferred model · Lab Preconditions Concurrency.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Python Defend the transferred model · Lab Preconditions Concurrency, keep Preconditions Concurrency connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Apply If-Match, If-None-Match, and related preconditions to prevent lost updates and duplicate effects. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

hints: For Python Defend the transferred model · Lab Preconditions Concurrency, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Preconditions Concurrency precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Python Defend the transferred model · Lab Preconditions Concurrency, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before running it: research archive client must repeat request P-2 after an uncertain timeout: GET status, POST job creation, PUT replacement, and PATCH transition are candidates. Classify safety, idempotency, body replay, and If-Match evidence before choosing. Competency probe: Apply If-Match, If-None-Match, and related preconditions to prevent lost updates and duplicate effects. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

For Python Defend the transferred model · Lab Preconditions Concurrency, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A client-side timestamp check prevents concurrent write conflicts. This remains false for Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers.
